channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Justin Trudeau dances at Taylor Swift concert amid destructive riot in Montreal, sparking outrage
item.description: Video of Justin Trudeau dancing at a Taylor Swift concert sparked outrage over the weekend after a destructive Montreal protest took place on the same night.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tens of thousands of Spaniards protest housing crunch and high rents in Barcelona
item.description: BARCELONA, Spain (AP) — Tens of thousands of Spaniards marched in downtown Barcelona on Saturday to protest the skyrocketing cost of renting an apartment in the popular tourist destination.
item.dc:creator: JOSEPH WILSON and HERNÁN MUÑOZ, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: As fast fashion's waste pollutes Africa's environment, designers in Ghana are finding a solution
item.description: ACCRA, Ghana (AP) — In a sprawling secondhand clothing market in Ghana’s capital, early morning shoppers jostle as they search through piles of garments, eager to pluck a bargain or a designer find from the stalls selling used and low-quality apparel imported from the West.
item.dc:creator: FRANCIS KOKUTSE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Former New York resident arrested over plan to support ISIS
item.description: An Albanian man with dual citizenship in the U.S. was charged Friday for attempting to provide material support to the Islamic State and distributing instructional material on making explosives, according to the Department of Justice (DOJ).
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Israeli-Moldovan rabbi living in UAE is missing. Israeli officials fear he may have been kidnapped
item.description: An Israeli-Moldovan rabbi living in the United Arab Emirates has gone missing, with Israeli authorities raising the suspicion he may have been kidnapped as tensions remain high with Iran.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Modi's party heads for victory in Maharashtra state election while opposition wins Jharkhand
item.description: NEW DELHI (AP) — Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s Hindu nationalist party headed for a victory Saturday in state elections in politically significant Maharashtra while the opposition won mineral-rich Jharkhand state.
item.dc:creator: ASHOK SHARMA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Canada's top military commander calls out US senator for questioning a woman's role in combat
item.description: The first woman to command Canada’s military is calling out a U.S. senator for questioning the role of women in combat.
item.dc:creator: Rob Gillies, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Philippine VP publicly threatens to have president assassinated
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — Philippine Vice President Sara Duterte said Saturday she has contracted an assassin to kill the president, his wife, and the House of Representatives speaker if she herself is killed, in a brazen public threat that she warned was not a joke.
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Prominent figure from Canada's trucker protests against COVID-19 restrictions found guilty
item.description: OTTAWA, Ontario (AP) — One of the most prominent figures from Canada's trucker protests against COVID-19 restrictions in 2022 has been found guilty on five counts including mischief and disobeying a court order.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pakistani city mourns 42 Shiite Muslims who were ambushed and killed in a gun attack
item.description: PESHAWAR, Pakistan (AP) — Protesters in Pakistan's restive northwest chanted anti-government slogans and tensions flared Friday after funeral prayers were held for 42 Shiite Muslims who were ambushed and killed by gunmen a day earlier in one of the region's deadliest such assaults in recent years.
item.dc:creator: RIAZ KHAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Police blow up suspicious package outside US Embassy in London
item.description: Law enforcement conducted a “controlled explosion” of a suspicious package outside of the U.S. Embassy in London on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Second Australian teen dies in tainted alcohol case in Laos that has killed 6 tourists
item.description: VANG VIENG, Laos (AP) — A second Australian teenager who fell critically ill after drinking tainted alcohol in Laos has died in a hospital in Bangkok, her family said Friday, bringing the death toll in the mass poisoning of foreign tourists to six.
item.dc:creator: JINTAMAS SAKSORNCHAI and DAVID RISING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Lawsuit against UK security agency by survivors of Ariana Grande concert bombing is rejected
item.description: LONDON (AP) — A legal claim against Britain’s domestic intelligence agency by more than 300 survivors of a bombing that killed 22 people at a 2017 Ariana Grande concert in Manchester was rejected Friday by a special tribunal. Judges on the U.K.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Indigenous leaders travel to UK from Peru to draw attention to oil damage and banking
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Indigenous leaders from the Wampis Nation in Peru are urging lawmakers at the House of Commons in London to ban international banks' support for Amazon oil activities they say harm their ancestral rainforests.
item.dc:creator: ED DAVEY and STEVEN GRATTAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel keeping its ‘eyes open’ for Iranian attacks during Trump transition period, ambassador says
item.description: Israel's Ambassador to the United Nations Danny Danon speaks to Fox News Digital about the threat from Iran during the Trump transition period.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: German ex-leader Merkel says she felt sorrow at Trump's comeback and recalls awkward non-handshake
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — Former German Chancellor Angela Merkel says she felt “sorrow” at Donald Trump's return to power and recalls that every meeting with him was “a competition: you or me.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Korea says Russia supplied air defense missiles to North Korea in return for its troops
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — Russia has supplied air defense missile systems to North Korea in exchange for sending its troops to support Russia's war efforts against Ukraine, a top South Korean official said Friday. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trudeau to cut sales tax and send checks to millions of Canadians as election looms
item.description: HALIFAX, Nova Scotia (AP) — Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau 's government announced plans Thursday to temporarily lift the federal sales tax off a number of items and send checks to millions of Canadians who are dealing with rising costs and as a federal election looms.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Death toll in Gaza from Israel-Hamas war passes 44,000, Palestinian officials say
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — The death toll in the Gaza Strip from the 13-month-old war between Israel and Hamas has surpassed 44,000, local health officials said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and FATMA KHALED, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Treasury imposes sanctions on Russian banks, financial officials
item.description: Russia’s third-largest bank, Gazprombank, and its subsidiaries were hit with U.S. sanctions Thursday, making it the largest bank not already targeted as the Russia-Ukraine war nears the three-year mark.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: ICC rejects Israeli appeals, issues arrest warrants for Benjamin Netanyahu, Yoav Gallant
item.description: The U.N.'s International Criminal Court approved arrest warrants for Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and former Defense Minister Yoav Gallant.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ukraine says Russia launched an intercontinental missile in an attack for the first time in the war
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said a missile used “matches the speed and altitude” of an ICBM. The Kremlin refused to comment on the strike.
item.dc:creator: Hanna Arhirova, WGN 9

item.title: Admiral: Middle East, Ukraine conflicts strain US air defense stocks
item.description: Conflicts in the Middle East and Ukraine are adding a strain to the U.S. military’s ability to respond to China, a top U.S. admiral warns.
item.dc:creator: Tanya Noury, NewsNation

item.title: Japan says it will watch China’s military activity after Beijing admits violating Japanese airspace
item.description: Japanese officials said they are watching to see if China prevents further airspace violations; China explained that an August violation of Japanese airspace was unintentional.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US vetoes UN resolution demanding a cease-fire in Gaza because there's no link to a hostage release
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — The United States on Wednesday vetoed a U.N. Security Council resolution demanding an immediate cease-fire in the war in Gaza because it was not linked to an immediate release of hostages taken captive by Hamas militants in Israel in October 2023.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Zelenskyy answers whether he's willing to cede Crimea, other territory in peace deal
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy tells Fox News that his country "cannot legally acknowledge any occupied territory of Ukraine as Russian.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Netanyahu offers $5 million to Palestinians for every Israeli hostage they help free
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu offers a $5 million reward for any Palestinian in Gaza who helps secure the freedom of Israeli hostages still held by Hamas after more than a year in captivity.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: After 97 UN aid trucks in Gaza are ‘violently looted’ by armed men, food prices soar
item.description: Food prices are rising in Gaza following the robbery of a U.N. convoy over the weekend, during which, drivers were forced to unload aid at gunpoint, officials say.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US Embassy in Kyiv closed as 'potential significant air attack' looms
item.description: The U.S. Embassy in Kyiv, Ukraine, has released a warning stating that they have "specific information of a potential significant air attack" taking place on Wednesday, Nov. 20.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden's Ukraine missile move will have 'some impact': Retired major
item.description: Lyons questioned the timing of the decision, saying, “I’m not sure why we didn’t allow this two or three years ago.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: India overtakes China as top source of international students at US colleges
item.description: For the 2023-2024 school year, India saw a massive 23 percent increase in the number of college students sent to America.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: From the Amazon rainforest, Biden declares nobody can reverse US progress on clean energy
item.description: MANAUS, Brazil (AP) — Speaking from the Amazon rainforest, President Joe Biden declared Sunday that there’s no going back in America’s “clean energy revolution” even as the incoming Trump administration vows to spur fossil fuel production and scale back efforts against climate change.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, GABRIELA SÁ PESSOA and COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Cruise line offers 4-year cruise to 'skip forward,' in wake of U.S. election results
item.description: One cruise line is offering travelers a customized journey for up to four years, to "skip forward," in the wake of the recent presidential election.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Australian zoo asks citizens to catch deadly spiders
item.description: Despite the zany ask, there is a valiant reason behind the call from The Australian Reptile Park in Sydney, New South Wales.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation
